items.headline: City releases youth services master plan and intervention programs report
items.summary: San José presented status updates on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, Youth Empowerment Alliance, and Youth Intervention Services Programs on April 9th. The report includes results from the Safe Summer Initiative Grant and provides insight into the city's youth programming and intervention efforts.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed, new date TBD
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street has been rescheduled from April 8 to a future date to be announced. Residents interested in this project should watch for a new hearing notice.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue highway-rail traffic signal project moves forward
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7th. This infrastructure project will improve traffic safety at the intersection of highway and rail operations on Bascom Avenue.

items.headline: City Updates Active Transportation Plan with New Scoring Criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is revising its Active Transportation Plan (which covers biking, walking, and transit) with Phase 2 updates and new evaluation methods. This plan affects future sidewalk, bike lane, and pedestrian infrastructure decisions across the city.

items.headline: City Council approves construction project with new contract award
items.summary: The council adopted plans and specifications for a construction project and awarded a contract to move it forward. This represents a physical infrastructure investment in Milpitas, though the specific project details are unclear from the agenda.

items.headline: Milpitas updates federal housing and community development plan
items.summary: The council considered a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan, which guides federal housing and community development funding. Amendments to this plan can affect what projects and programs the city prioritizes for housing, economic development, and public services.

items.headline: 65-unit senior housing complex approved on East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a nearly $9M senior apartment complex with 65 units at 678 E St John St, addressing the region's growing need for affordable senior housing. This is one of the larger senior-focused developments recently approved in San José.

items.headline: City Council approves emergency interim housing siting policy
items.summary: The council voted on a new emergency interim housing siting policy on April 1st, addressing where temporary housing for unhoused residents can be located. This policy change affects where the city can place emergency shelters and temporary housing facilities across San José.
